title: Bundle Pots
description: Use decorated pots as bundles. Contents they had stored inside will remain intact when breaking the pot without destroying (e.g. with your fist). They can store different stacks of items the same way bundles were able to.
body:
# what is it
- A decorated pot can now hold up to one stack of different items, exactly like the bundle e.g. 32 wheat seeds and 32 stones or 4 ender perls, 4 eggs and 32 stone slabs. Any more of the previous mentioned items won't be accepted. There can be as much as 64 different types of items. If there are other mods with non-vanilla stack-size that are powers of 2 e.g. 4, 8 or 32, this should also work.
- A decorated pot will retain its items when it doesn't shatter.  Mine the pot with a pickaxe, axe, shovel, hoe or sword not enchanted with Silk Touch to get the items back. To move the items, mine with any other tool or a tool from above enchanted with Silk Touch. See [Decorated Pot](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Decorated_Pot)
- A gui will never come because I like it having no gui.
# how to install
1. install [https://fabricmc.net/use/installer/](fabric)
2. in your .minecraft folder, create a directory named "mods"
3. put in this directory the latest version for the snapshot you're installing this app for of [Fabric API](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api)
4. put this mod into the folder, check correct versions
# port to forge
is not planned as no experience with forge and with the move to neoforge, I was left behind. If you want to port it to forge, feel free to do so
# license and source of the icon
[source](https://minecraft.wiki/images/thumb/Decorated_Pot_%28N%29_JE2_BE2.png/150px-Decorated_Pot_%28N%29_JE2_BE2.png?1209f) and [license](https://www.minecraft.net/en-us/eula)
